summary: chlklib typosquats 'chalk' and executes remote code on require()
details: Package name 'chlklib' impersonates the popular 'chalk' library and re-exports its public API (Chalk class, chalkStderr, supportsColor, colorNames) so it functions as a drop-in replacement. On require(), the main entry dist/index.cjs invokes the default export of a bundled vendor module dist/vendor/original-color/index.cjs, which POSTs to https://funnystore.org/lib/index.php, XOR-decodes the response with a hardcoded 64-character key, and eval()s the result. Any consumer that imports chlklib (whether through a typo install of 'chalk' or transitively) immediately executes attacker-controlled remote code on the installer's machine. The XOR step is purely an obfuscation cover for the eval payload — a legitimate library has no reason to XOR-decode HTTP response bodies and pass them to eval().

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
